input.title: Point of Sale and Retail Management
input.description: Point of Sale and Retail Management is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with React and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Node.js, PostgreSQL, Redis for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Product Management with Barcode
requirements: The system must provide end-to-end product management with barcode capabilities for creating, updating, viewing, and retiring relevant business records.
Product Management with Barcode should support role-based permissions, validation rules, and duplicate-prevention checks where appropriate.
Users must be able to search, filter, and review historical activity related to product management with barcode without leaving the module context.
The platform should maintain auditability for important actions performed within product management with barcode and retain operational history for reporting.

FR-03.title: Inventory Tracking
requirements: The system must capture inventory tracking data in real time or near real time as operational events occur.
Inventory Tracking should provide a timeline, status history, or change log so users can understand what happened and when.
Users must be able to review, filter, and reconcile inventory tracking records against the related business entities.
The platform should flag missing, inconsistent, or exception conditions that require attention during inventory tracking.

FR-04.title: Multi-store Inventory Sync
requirements: The platform must support multi-store inventory sync through stable service contracts with external systems or internal integration layers.
Multi-store Inventory Sync should validate inbound and outbound payloads before data is accepted or shared with downstream systems.
Failures, retries, and reconciliation outcomes for multi-store inventory sync must be observable through logs, alerts, and operational dashboards.
Credentials, tokens, and connection settings used by multi-store inventory sync must be managed securely and rotated according to policy.

FR-01.title: Plan Product Management with Barcode Workflow and Contracts
FR-01.description: Review the SRS requirement bullets for product management with barcode and break them into executable API, UI, data, security, and reporting subtasks. Define the workflow states, user roles, field-level validations, request/response contracts, and failure cases before implementation starts. Use these requirement drivers as the baseline: The system must provide end-to-end product management with barcode capabilities for creating, updating, viewing, and retiring relevant business records; Product Management with Barcode should support role-based permissions, validation rules, and duplicate-prevention checks where appropriate; Users must be able to search, filter, and review historical activity related to product management with barcode without leaving the module context.
acceptance_criteria: The end-to-end workflow for product management with barcode is documented from trigger to completion
Validation rules, role restrictions, and error scenarios for product management with barcode are explicitly defined
Implementation subtasks for product management with barcode can be picked up independently by engineering

FR-01.title: Implement Product Management with Barcode Backend Services
FR-01.description: Build the backend services for product management with barcode on React, Node.js, PostgreSQL, Redis. Create route handlers or service endpoints, model the core business operations, enforce validation and authorization rules, persist workflow state changes, and add structured error handling and audit logging. Make sure the implementation covers these behaviors: The system must provide end-to-end product management with barcode capabilities for creating, updating, viewing, and retiring relevant business records; Product Management with Barcode should support role-based permissions, validation rules, and duplicate-prevention checks where appropriate; Users must be able to search, filter, and review historical activity related to product management with barcode without leaving the module context.
acceptance_criteria: Core product management with barcode business rules are implemented for Point of Sale and Retail Management
Validation, authorization, and error handling are covered for product management with barcode
API/service behavior for product management with barcode is testable and documented for integration

FR-01.title: Implement Product Management with Barcode Frontend Flow
FR-01.description: Build the frontend flow for product management with barcode with the exact forms, tables, filters, wizards, status indicators, and action buttons required by the SRS. Connect the UI to the backend contract, handle optimistic updates or refresh flows where needed, and provide user-facing validation, permissions feedback, loading states, and recovery states.
acceptance_criteria: The product management with barcode user flow is accessible and responsive across supported browsers
Forms, filters, tables, and feedback states for product management with barcode behave correctly
The product management with barcode UI is fully integrated with backend APIs and handles loading and error states

FR-01.title: Design Product Management with Barcode Persistence Model
FR-01.description: Design the persistence layer for product management with barcode. Identify the core entities, relationships, indexes, status fields, history/audit tables, and reporting attributes required to support the workflow. Add migrations and seed or fixture data where that helps engineering validate the feature early.
acceptance_criteria: The persistence model for product management with barcode supports required workflows and reporting needs

FR-01.title: Harden Product Management with Barcode Security and Access Rules
FR-01.description: Apply security controls to product management with barcode. Implement role checks, field-level protections, data masking where appropriate, secure defaults, audit trails, and abuse-prevention rules. Verify that sensitive operations are guarded both in the API layer and in the UI visibility rules.

Sensitive operations and data paths in product management with barcode are audited and protected against misuse

FR-01.title: Write Product Management with Barcode Automated and End-to-End Tests
FR-01.description: Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for product management with barcode. Cover valid flows, invalid inputs, permission failures, state transitions, data integrity checks, and regression-prone edge cases pulled from the SRS. Add fixtures or mocks that make the feature verifiable in CI and local development.
